title: 10 best herbal essential oils for allergies
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for allergies are plant-based extracts used to alleviate symptoms associated with allergic reactions, such as itching, sneezing, and congestion. They help by reducing inflammation, opening airways, and promoting relaxation. The best herbal essential oils for allergies include Eucalyptus globulus, Melaleuca alternifolia, Lavandula angustifolia, Thymus vulgaris, Rosmarinus officinalis, Pelargonium graveolens, Echinacea purpurea, Cymbopogon citratus, Zingiber officinale, and Sambucus nigra. If left untreated, allergies can severely impact daily life, causing missed workdays, disrupted sleep patterns, and strained relationships due to constant discomfort and stress.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower essential oils helps with allergies because its potent anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated airways, reducing congestion and discomfort. The oil's natural antihistamine effects also help to block histamine receptors, minimizing allergic reactions and relieving symptoms like itching and sneezing. Additionally, the oil's antioxidant properties help to combat oxidative stress, further alleviating allergy-related inflammation and promoting overall respiratory health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Echinacea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cichoric acid and caffeic acid, can help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often a contributing factor to allergic reactions.

Immune system support: Echinacea has been shown to stimulate the immune system, increasing the production of white blood cells and activating natural killer cells, which can help fight off allergens and reduce symptoms of allergies.

Antioxidant activity: Echinacea's antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which is important for overall health and may help alleviate allergy symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of the Kansas coneflower have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and reduce inflammation associated with allergic reactions.
Seeds: The seeds of the Kansas coneflower contain anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and congestion caused by allergies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia flowers and leaves in sufficient quantity for distillation.
Cut the harvested plant material into small pieces to facilitate steam distillation and blend with water.
Combine 500 grams of plant material with 1000 milliliters of distilled water in a large distillation apparatus.
Steam distill the plant mixture at atmospheric pressure and ambient temperature for 2-3 hours.
Collect and filter the essential oil resulting from steam distillation, storing it in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White oak essential oils helps with allergies because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe irritated tissues and reduce congestion. The oil's natural antioxidants also help combat free radicals, which can exacerbate allergy symptoms like sneezing and runny nose. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help to eliminate allergens like dust mites and mold from the environment, providing a more comprehensive solution for alleviating allergy symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White oak essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease congestion caused by allergic reactions.

Antihistamine-like activity: White oak essential oil has been shown to exhibit antihistamine-like properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and runny nose associated with allergies.
Antioxidant: White oak essential oil's antioxidant properties can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by environmental allergens, reducing inflammation and promoting overall health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a wealth of bioactive compounds, white oak roots are used to create essential oils that help soothe and calm allergy symptoms.
Barks: The bark of white oak contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are extracted to produce essential oils with anti-inflammatory properties to alleviate allergic reactions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 pounds of fresh white oak leaves and stems from mature trees.
Dry the plant material in a warm oven at 150°f for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material using 50 gallons of water for 8 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a rotary evaporator at 180°f.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter and store it in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with allergies because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated skin, reducing redness and itching associated with allergic reactions. The calming scent of lavender also relaxes the body, decreasing stress levels which can exacerbate allergy symptoms. Additionally, lavender oil's antimicrobial properties help to combat fungal and bacterial infections often found in allergy-prone areas, further alleviating discomfort and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English Lavender essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and itching associated with allergic reactions, making it a natural remedy for skin irritations and respiratory issues.

Antihistamine: Lavender oil's antihistamine effects can help neutralize histamine, the chemical released during an allergic reaction, which in turn reduces symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and congestion.

Calming and sedative: English Lavender essential oil has a calming and sedative effect on the body, which can help reduce anxiety and stress related to allergies, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which help to calm and soothe allergic reactions.
Flowers: The flowers of the English lavender plant are rich in linalool, a natural antihistamine that helps to relieve symptoms of hay fever and other allergies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature lavender flowers from your garden or local supplier weighing approximately 100g.
Dry the harvested lavender flowers for 7-10 days in a warm and well-ventilated area.
Steam distill the dried lavender flowers using a clevenger apparatus with a yield of 20-25%.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separation funnel and store it in a dark glass bottle.
Filter the extracted lavandula angustifolia essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities weighing approximately 10ml.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with allergies because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and congestion caused by allergic reactions. The oil's antihistamine-like effects also help to alleviate itching, sneezing, and runny noses. Additionally, thyme essential oil has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections that often accompany allergies, such as sinusitis and bronchitis. By inhaling or applying topically, thyme essential oil can provide effective relief from allergy symptoms, promoting a sense of calm and comfort for those affected.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine property: Thyme essential oil has a strong antihistamine effect, which helps to reduce allergic reactions and alleviate symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and congestion.

Anti-inflammatory property: Thyme essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling and ease the discomfort associated with allergies, making it an effective natural remedy for skin irritations and respiratory issues.

Antioxidant property: Thyme essential oil's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to allergic reactions, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall immune system health.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Thyme flowers contain a high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe allergic reactions and reduce inflammation.
Roots: Thyme roots are rich in phenolic compounds, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of allergies such as itching and redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and stems from organic farms.
Dry the harvested material in well-ventilated areas for 7 days.
Steam distill the dried thymus vulgaris plant material using a clevenger apparatus.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through cheesecloth or paper filters.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with allergies because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe irritated skin and reduce redness. By using herbal lemon balm essential oils topically or through aromatherapy, individuals can find relief from itchy skin, sneezing, and congestion caused by allergies.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The essential oil of herbal lemon balm contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling, redness, and discomfort associated with allergic reactions.

Calming and sedative effects: Herbal lemon balm essential oil has a calming and sedative effect on the body, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety often triggered by allergies, promoting relaxation and reducing overall allergy symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, lemon balm roots help soothe allergic reactions by reducing inflammation and calming irritated skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant contain compounds like rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate allergy symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves and flowers ensuring freshness for optimal oil yield.
Dry the plant material in a well-ventilated area or using a dehydrator at low temperature setting.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and steam distill it using a still or a commercial distillation kit.
Collect the distilled essential oil and transfer it to dark glass bottles for storage and preservation.
Allow the oil to mature for several weeks before use or blend with other oils to enhance its therapeutic benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with allergies because of its potent anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as eucalyptol and globul, work to reduce swelling and ease breathing congestion caused by allergens. Additionally, the oil's natural antimicrobial properties help to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can exacerbate allergic reactions. By using Tasmanian blue gum essential oils, individuals may experience relief from symptoms of allergies such as itchiness, sneezing, and runny eyes.
remedy_properties: Decongestant: Eucalyptus globulus essential oil helps to relieve nasal congestion and sinus pressure, making it a natural remedy for allergy sufferers who experience sinus issues.
Anti-inflammatory: The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and itching caused by allergic reactions, such as hives or skin rashes.
Antimicrobial: Eucalyptus globulus essential oil has antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections caused by allergies, such as ear infections or sinus infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum plant are valued for their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate allergy symptoms due to their high content of anti-inflammatory compounds.
Barks: The barks of the plant contain bioactive compounds that help to calm down allergic reactions, making it an effective ingredient in essential oils for allergies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated, collecting 20% of the total plant biomass.
Dry the harvested eucalyptus globulus leaves by spreading them thinly on a paper bag or cloth sheet for 7-10 days, monitoring moisture levels daily.
Steam distill the dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in a copper still using 30 gallons of distilled water and 1 gallon of plant material over 2 hours, collecting condensate into a separate vessel.
Filter the resulting essential oil from the condensate using a coffee filter or paper towel, discarding any impurities and retaining 10 milliliters of clear oil.
Store the eucalyptus globulus essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, refrigerating at 39°f to 45°f for up to 6 months, using as needed for allergy relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with allergies because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin and airways. The oil contains flavonoids and carotenoids that have potent antioxidant effects, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with allergies such as itching, redness, and swelling. Additionally, pot marigold essential oil has a gentle, non-irritating properties that make it an effective natural remedy for relieving allergy-related discomforts, promoting healthy skin and respiratory well-being.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine property: This property helps to reduce histamine levels in the body, which can alleviate symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and runny nose associated with allergic reactions.

Anti-inflammatory property: Pot Marigold essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling, redness, and irritation caused by allergic responses, providing relief from skin and respiratory issues.

Antioxidant property: As an antioxidant, Pot Marigold essential oil helps to neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate allergy symptoms such as congestion, fatigue, and joint pain.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers at peak blooming time for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers using air circulation or desiccation methods within 24 hours.
Combine 100 grams of dried calendula flowers with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for maximum extraction and infusion.
Filter the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion essential oils helps with allergies because its potent anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and alleviate congestion in the nasal passages, sinuses, and respiratory tract. The oil's antioxidant compounds also work to neutralize histamine release, effectively calming down allergic reactions. Additionally, dandelion oil's natural expectorant properties help to loosen and expel excess mucus, further easing allergy symptoms such as coughing, sneezing, and congestion. Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds can reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with allergies, providing relief from symptoms like skin rashes and hives.

Immunomodulatory Effects: Dandelion essential oil has been shown to modulate the immune system, which can help regulate allergic responses and prevent the overactive release of histamine, a chemical involved in allergic reactions.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers and leaves on a sunny morning for maximum potency.
Dry the plant material using air circulation or desiccant for 24 hours to reduce moisture levels.
Crush dried herbs into smaller particles using a mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Steam distill the crushed plant material in a still with 20ml of water for 30 minutes.
Filter the resulting oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove impurities and sediment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium essential oils helps with allergies because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe irritated tissues and reduce swelling. Additionally, geranium oil has decongestant properties that help to clear nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier for the body to expel allergens. Its antihistamine-like effects also help to alleviate symptoms such as itching, redness, and hives. Antihistamine-like activity: The chemical composition of Geranium essential oil exhibits antihistamine-like activity, which can help counteract the effects of histamine release during an allergic reaction, reducing symptoms such as congestion and itching.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help to soothe and calm allergic reactions, making them a popular choice for natural allergy relief.
Seeds: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms associated with allergies, such as itching and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pelargonium graveolens leaves at dawn when the plant is fully hydrated, collecting approximately 100g of leaf material.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer on paper towels or screens for 24-48 hours to reduce moisture content to 5%.
Place the dried pelargonium graveolens leaves in a stainless steel distillation apparatus and add water at a ratio of 1:3, stirring well.
Heat the mixture in the distillation apparatus at 100°c for 2 hours or until 20ml of essential oil is collected, using a condenser to capture the steam.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities and store it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort essential oils helps with allergies because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The oil has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms such as itching, redness, and swelling associated with allergic reactions. When applied topically or inhaled, the oil reduces histamine production, soothing irritated skin and airways, providing relief from congestion, sneezing, and runny nose. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that exacerbate allergy symptoms, promoting a balanced and healthy response to allergens.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: St John's Wort essential oil has natural antihistamine properties, which helps to reduce the effects of histamine release during an allergic reaction, such as itching and sneezing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in St John's Wort essential oil can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages and sinuses caused by allergies, providing relief from congestion and sinus pressure.

Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort essential oil contains antioxidant compounds that help to protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate allergic reactions and promote inflammation.

Flowers: Contain hyperforin, a natural antihistamine that helps to relieve itching, redness, and swelling associated with allergic reactions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh st. john's wort leaves and flowers at dawn on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Dry the plant material in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator set at 35°c for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material with 500ml of water for 3 hours to extract the essential oil, collecting the distilled liquid as it exits the still.
Allow the resulting essential oil to settle and separate from any impurities or debris for 2 hours before decanting into dark glass containers.
Store the final hypericum perforatum essential oil in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight, using within 6 months of preparation for optimal therapeutic benefits.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat allergies with herbal essential oils is to blend lavender, peppermint, and eucalyptus oils with a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba. Apply the blend topically to affected areas or diffuse it in the air. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of these herbs help soothe allergic reactions, while the menthol in peppermint oil helps clear congestion. Regular use can reduce symptoms and alleviate discomfort associated with allergies.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to allergies that are treated with herbal essential oils are environmental sensitivities, intolerances, and irritations. For instance, certain essential oils like lavender, peppermint, and eucalyptus can help alleviate symptoms of hay fever, sinusitis, and congestion. Additionally, herbal essential oils such as chamomile, calendula, and tea tree oil have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe skin irritations, itchiness, and rashes caused by allergies or sensitivity reactions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with allergies because of its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compound, eucalyptol, opens up airways and relieves congestion, making it easier to breathe. It also reduces inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, which helps alleviate symptoms of allergies such as runny nose, sneezing, and itchy eyes. By promoting a healthy respiratory system and reducing inflammation, Eucalyptus globulus essential oils provides natural relief from allergy symptoms, allowing for a more comfortable and symptom-free life.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: It helps with allergies by acting as an expectorant, relieving congestion and facilitating the removal of mucus from the respiratory tract.
Bornyl acetate: It helps with allergies by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and discomfort in the nasal passages and sinuses.
α-pinene: It helps with allergies by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for triggering allergic reactions, thus providing relief from symptoms such as itching and sneezing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary part used to make Eucalyptus essential oil, which is known for its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate allergy symptoms.
Barks: The bark of Eucalyptus globulus is also used to make essential oils, which are rich in compounds that help reduce inflammation and ease respiratory issues associated with allergies.
Buds: Eucalyptus buds are another part used to create essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, helping to alleviate allergy symptoms such as congestion and itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves from the plant's upper branches during the summer months when the leaves are most fragrant.
Steam distill the harvested leaves in a copper still at 100°c for 2-3 hours to release the oil from the plant material.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a separate container and filter it using a paper filter to remove any impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and keep it in a cool place to prevent oxidation.
Label the bottle with the oil's name, concentration, and date of production, and store it in a safe place away from heat sources.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with allergies because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil, also known as tea tree oil, has been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for allergic reactions. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help combat infections that can exacerbate allergies, such as sinusitis and bronchitis. By addressing the underlying causes of allergies, Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils can provide relief and support for individuals suffering from seasonal or environmental allergies.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (also known as eucalyptol): This terpene helps with allergies by acting as a bronchodilator, reducing inflammation in the airways and making it easier to breathe.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene oxide has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate symptoms associated with allergies such as congestion and skin irritation.
Linalool: This monoterpene alcohol has a calming effect on the body, reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of allergic reactions such as itching and redness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary source of Tea Tree essential oil due to their high concentration of cineole, a compound known for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate allergy symptoms.

Stems: Some essential oil manufacturers also use the stems of the Melaleuca alternifolia plant, which contain a high amount of cineole, similar to the leaves, making it a secondary source for allergy-related essential oil production.

Buds: Tea Tree buds are another part of the plant used to produce essential oil, which is rich in cineole and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate allergy symptoms by reducing inflammation and fighting infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers at dawn when essential oils are most concentrated.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100°c for 2 hours to separate essential oils from water.
Separate the essential oils from water and impurities through decantation and filtration processes.
Collect and store the essential oils in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids to preserve their quality.
Age the essential oils for 24 hours before use to allow the flavors and aromas to mature fully.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with allergies because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The oil's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax airways, providing relief from allergy symptoms. Additionally, its calming effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety associated with allergic reactions, promoting a sense of relaxation and well-being. remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, reducing the body's allergic response and alleviating symptoms of allergies such as itching and congestion.

Linalyl acetate: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to reduce the production of histamine, a chemical responsible for allergic reactions, and alleviate symptoms such as itching and sneezing.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used to make essential oils for allergies as they contain the same chemical compounds as the flowers, but are often more readily available and less expensive.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning when the dew has evaporated completely naturally.
Dry the harvested flowers thoroughly in a warm dark place for about 7 to 10 days slowly.
Steam distill the dried flowers in a distillation apparatus for about 2 to 3 hours continuously.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a clean dark glass container for storage later.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities completely.

Flowers: Thyme flowers are used for essential oil production as they contain a higher concentration of carvacrol, a natural anti-inflammatory that helps reduce congestion and itching associated with allergies.

Stems: Thyme stems are also used for essential oil production, as they contain a combination of thymol and carvacrol, making them effective in alleviating allergy symptoms such as congestion and itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves by hand on a sunny day in summer morning.
Steam distill the fresh thymus vulgaris leaves at 100°c for 2 hours to release oil.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus in a glass bottle over 2 hours.
Filter the essential oil using a coffee filter paper to remove impurities within 1 hour.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle in a cool place for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with allergies because of its potent anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The oil's main compound, camphor, has been shown to reduce nasal congestion and ease respiratory issues associated with allergies. remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 kg of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves at dawn when the dew is still present.
Steam distill the leaves in a 30:1 ratio with water at 40-60°c for 2-3 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separating funnel and refrigerate the oil at 5°c.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter to remove any impurities and store in a dark glass bottle.
Analyze the final product for its chemical composition and quality before labeling and packaging for distribution.

Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separator or decanter.

Icariin: Exhibits anti-allergic properties by inhibiting the release of histamine, a key player in allergic responses.
Caffeic acid: Possesses antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation caused by allergies.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate allergy symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain alkaloids, including echinacin, which have antihistamine and anti-inflammatory effects that help combat allergies.
Roots: The roots are used because they are rich in polyacetylenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help alleviate allergy symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers in the morning to ensure optimal oil content.
Steam distill the harvested flowers at 100°c for 2 hours using a 1:10 flower-to-water ratio.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water phase using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron membrane filter to remove impurities and improve clarity.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle at 4°c to preserve its potency and shelf life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps with allergies because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The oil, also known as lemongrass oil, has a calming effect on the body, reducing inflammation and itching associated with allergic reactions. Its decongestant properties help to clear nasal passages, relieving congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, the oil's antimicrobial properties help to fight off infections that can exacerbate allergic symptoms, providing relief and promoting overall well-being. This natural remedy can be a valuable tool in managing allergy symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Citral: Helps alleviate allergy symptoms by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that reduce histamine release and oxidative stress.
Limonene: Exhibits immunomodulatory effects, which help regulate the immune system's response to allergens and reduce inflammation.
Geranial: Displays antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, thereby inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and reducing allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary part used for making essential oils due to their high concentration of limonene and linalool, which have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties.
Stems: The stems of Cymbopogon citratus are also used for essential oil production, as they contain a high amount of citral, a compound that helps alleviate allergy symptoms.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, of Cymbopogon citratus are used for essential oil production as they contain a high amount of geraniol and citronellal, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Steam distill the fresh leaves in a copper still at 100-150 psi pressure for 30 minutes.
Collect the distilled oil in a separate container and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight and heat sources for up to 12 months.
Label and date the bottle and store it in a cool dry place for future use as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with allergies because of its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. It has been traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues, such as congestion and coughing, which are common symptoms of allergies. The oil's active compounds, gingerols and shogaols, have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce swelling and ease breathing. Additionally, Zingiber officinale has been shown to possess antioxidant properties, further supporting its potential to alleviate allergic reactions and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which helps in reducing the allergic response and inflammation associated with allergies.
Gingerol: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help in soothing the allergic symptoms, such as itching and redness, and also has anti-histamine effects.

Shogaol: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and anti-histamine properties that help in reducing the allergic response and inflammation associated with allergies, and also has antioxidant effects to protect against oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most commonly used part of ginger for essential oil production, as it contains the highest concentration of bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: The roots of ginger are also widely used for essential oil production, as they contain similar bioactive compounds to the rhyzomes, which help alleviate allergy symptoms.
Leaves: Ginger leaves are sometimes used for essential oil production, as they contain smaller amounts of bioactive compounds that still provide some anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest zingiber officinale rhizomes by digging them up when the leaves start to yellow in late summer after 3 months of growth.
Wash the harvested rhizomes thoroughly with clean water to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill 100g of washed rhizomes in 500ml of water for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Store the extracted zingiber officinale essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra essential oils helps with allergies because its active compound, isoquercitrin, has potent anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. This natural oil works by blocking the release of histamine, a chemical that triggers allergic reactions. Its soothing effects also help to reduce congestion and inflammation in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. The oil's ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines also helps to alleviate symptoms associated with seasonal allergies, providing natural relief and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Oleuropein: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with allergic reactions.
Quercetin: Exhibits potent antioxidant and antihistamine properties, which help to neutralize histamine release and alleviate symptoms of allergies.
Luteolin: Possesses anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, helping to regulate the body's immune response and reduce allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Sambucus nigra flowers are used to make essential oils due to their antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate allergy symptoms.
Leaves: Sambucus nigra leaves are used to make essential oils due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation associated with allergies.
Stems: Sambucus nigra stems are used to make essential oils due to their antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate symptoms of allergies such as congestion and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200 grams of fresh sambucus nigra flowers when they are in full bloom for optimal oil content.
Dry the flowers in a clean environment away from direct sunlight for 7-10 days to reduce moisture.
Use a commercial steam distillation unit to extract the essential oil from dried flowers at a temperature of 80-100°c.
Collect the essential oil after 2-3 hours of distillation when the distillate has reached a volume of 10-15 milliliters.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or a paper filter to remove any impurities and sediment.
